Window Went Off-Screen on Mac or Windows? How to Pull It Back
Lately I keep ending up with windows that drift off-screen, so I'm capturing the fix.
This is easy on Windows. Whatever the window is — Chrome, Office, some popup — once it's off-screen you can rescue it with a key combo.
Focus the window first (Alt+Tab to it), then press
. A small menu appears (critical — you have to focus the window first, otherwise this won't work):
don't try to grab the window with your mouse
at this point. After picking Move, press any arrow key once to "activate" the move. Now you can move your mouse around and the window follows the cursor — drag it back into view.
Mac doesn't have an equivalent menu. The easiest fix is just Command+Q twice to force-quit the app, then reopen — the window comes back at a default position.
If you want to keep the window state, click the menu bar "Window" → "Zoom" to snap it back into a sensible size and position:

Sometimes a window drifts off your screen and you can't grab it with the mouse. This post covers two quick fixes — Alt + Space → Move + arrow keys on Windows to drag any off-screen window back, and the Window → Zoom menu (or force-quit) on Mac.
